description: Phase 2 Write Hooks + Transaction Flow Engine — useMutation hooks for all borrow/SP operations, Jotai flow state machine, flow dialog UI for multi-step tx feedback

userStories.title: Create flow state atom and types
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a Jotai atom that tracks transaction flow state so the UI can show multi-step progress feedback.
acceptanceCriteria: Create packages/web3/src/features/borrow/atoms/flow-atoms.ts
Define BorrowFlowState type: { flowId: string, operation: string, steps: FlowStep[], currentStepIndex: number, account: string }
Define FlowStep type: { id: string, label: string, status: 'idle' | 'pending' | 'confirming' | 'confirmed' | 'error', txHash?: string, error?: { name: string | null, message: string } }
borrowFlowAtom — use atomWithStorage from jotai/utils with key 'mento:borrow:flow' for localStorage persistence, default null
Export borrowFlowAtom, BorrowFlowState, FlowStep types from atoms/index.ts barrel
Typecheck passes (pnpm check-types)

userStories.title: Create flow execution engine
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a function that orchestrates multi-step transaction flows, updating the flow atom at each stage.
acceptanceCriteria: Create packages/web3/src/features/borrow/tx-flows/engine.ts
Define FlowStepDefinition type: { id: string, label: string, buildTx: () => Promise<CallParams | null> } — null means skip step

executeFlow(wagmiConfig, setFlowAtom, flowId, operation, account, stepDefs) function that: sets initial flow state (all steps idle), iterates steps sequentially, updates status to pending then confirming (with txHash) then confirmed, stops on error with error details
Uses sendSdkTransaction and waitForTx from tx-flows/send-tx.ts (already exists from Phase 0)
Skips steps where buildTx returns null (marks them confirmed immediately with label like 'Skipped - not needed')
Returns { success: boolean, txHashes: string[] }
Export executeFlow and FlowStepDefinition from tx-flows/index.ts
Typecheck passes (pnpm check-types)
userStories.notes: setFlowAtom is a Jotai setter function (from useSetAtom). The engine updates the atom at each state transition so the UI reacts in real-time.

userStories.title: Create use-open-trove write hook
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a hook that handles the full open-trove flow: check allowance, approve if needed, open trove.
acceptanceCriteria: Create packages/web3/src/features/borrow/hooks/use-open-trove.ts
Uses useMutation from @tanstack/react-query
mutationFn accepts { symbol, params: OpenTroveParams, wagmiConfig } — OpenTroveParams from SDK includes owner, ownerIndex, collAmount, boldAmount, annualInterestRate, maxUpfrontFee
Uses executeFlow to orchestrate: step 1 = check collateral allowance and approve if insufficient (buildTx returns null if allowance sufficient), step 2 = sdk.buildOpenTroveTransaction(symbol, params)
Uses useBorrowService() for SDK access, useSetAtom(borrowFlowAtom) for flow state
onSuccess: invalidates query keys ['borrow', 'userTroves'], ['borrow', 'allowance'], ['borrow', 'branchStats'] via useQueryClient
onSuccess: shows toast notification
Export from hooks/index.ts
Typecheck passes (pnpm check-types)

userStories.title: Create use-adjust-trove write hook
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a hook for adjusting an existing trove (add/remove collateral, borrow/repay).
acceptanceCriteria: Create packages/web3/src/features/borrow/hooks/use-adjust-trove.ts
Uses useMutation
mutationFn accepts { symbol, params: AdjustTroveParams, wagmiConfig } — AdjustTroveParams from SDK includes troveId, collChange, isCollIncrease, debtChange, isDebtIncrease, maxUpfrontFee
Uses executeFlow: step 1 = approve collateral if adding (buildTx returns null if removing or allowance sufficient), step 2 = sdk.buildAdjustTroveTransaction(symbol, params)
onSuccess: invalidates ['borrow', 'userTroves'], ['borrow', 'troveData'], ['borrow', 'allowance'], ['borrow', 'branchStats']
onSuccess: shows toast
Export from hooks/index.ts
Typecheck passes (pnpm check-types)

userStories.title: Create use-close-trove write hook
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a hook for closing a trove (repay debt, receive collateral).
acceptanceCriteria: Create packages/web3/src/features/borrow/hooks/use-close-trove.ts
Uses useMutation
mutationFn accepts { symbol, troveId, wagmiConfig }
Uses executeFlow: step 1 = check debt token allowance and approve if needed, step 2 = sdk.buildCloseTroveTransaction(symbol, troveId)
onSuccess: invalidates ['borrow', 'userTroves'], ['borrow', 'troveData'], ['borrow', 'branchStats']
onSuccess: shows toast, sets borrowViewAtom to 'dashboard'
Export from hooks/index.ts
Typecheck passes (pnpm check-types)

userStories.title: Create use-adjust-interest-rate and use-claim-collateral hooks
userStories.description: As a developer, I need hooks for single-tx operations: changing interest rate and claiming surplus collateral.
acceptanceCriteria: Create packages/web3/src/features/borrow/hooks/use-adjust-interest-rate.ts
useAdjustInterestRate mutation accepts { symbol, troveId, newRate, maxUpfrontFee, wagmiConfig }
Single-step flow: sdk.buildAdjustInterestRateTransaction(symbol, troveId, newRate, maxUpfrontFee) → send → wait
onSuccess: invalidates ['borrow', 'troveData'], ['borrow', 'userTroves'], ['borrow', 'interestRateBrackets']
Create packages/web3/src/features/borrow/hooks/use-claim-collateral.ts
useClaimCollateral mutation accepts { symbol, wagmiConfig }
Single-step flow: sdk.buildClaimCollateralTransaction(symbol) → send → wait
onSuccess: invalidates ['borrow', 'userTroves']
Both show toast on success and use executeFlow for consistent flow atom updates
Export both from hooks/index.ts
Typecheck passes (pnpm check-types)

userStories.title: Create Stability Pool transaction builders
userStories.description: As a developer, I need functions that build SP deposit/withdraw transactions since the SDK doesn't cover stability pools.
acceptanceCriteria: Create packages/web3/src/features/borrow/stability-pool/tx-builders.ts

buildSpDeposit(spAddress, amount, doClaim) — uses viem encodeFunctionData with stabilityPoolAbi to encode provideToSP(uint256, bool), returns CallParams: { to: spAddress, data: encoded, value: '0x0' }
buildSpWithdraw(spAddress, amount, doClaim) — encodes withdrawFromSP(uint256, bool), returns CallParams
CallParams format must be compatible with sendSdkTransaction (strings for to/data/value)
Export from stability-pool/index.ts
Typecheck passes (pnpm check-types)
userStories.notes: Use encodeFunctionData from viem. stabilityPoolAbi is already at features/borrow/stability-pool/abi.ts from Phase 0.

userStories.title: Create use-sp-deposit and use-sp-withdraw write hooks
userStories.description: As a developer, I need write hooks for depositing into and withdrawing from the Stability Pool.
acceptanceCriteria: Create packages/web3/src/features/borrow/hooks/use-sp-deposit.ts
useSpDeposit mutation accepts { symbol, amount, doClaim, wagmiConfig }
Resolves SP address via useStabilityPoolAddress hook or inline SDK resolution
Uses executeFlow: step 1 = check debt token allowance for SP address, approve if needed, step 2 = buildSpDeposit → send → wait
onSuccess: invalidates stability pool queries (['borrow', 'stabilityPool'], ['borrow', 'stabilityPoolStats'])
Create packages/web3/src/features/borrow/hooks/use-sp-withdraw.ts
useSpWithdraw mutation accepts { symbol, amount, doClaim, wagmiConfig }
Single-step flow: buildSpWithdraw → send → wait (no approval needed)
onSuccess: invalidates stability pool queries
Both show toast on success
Export both from hooks/index.ts
Typecheck passes (pnpm check-types)
userStories.notes: SP address resolved via useStabilityPoolAddress from Phase 1. For mutation hooks that need the address, either pass it as a param or resolve it inside mutationFn.

userStories.title: Create flow step component
userStories.description: As a user, I want each transaction step to show its status clearly with icons and explorer links.
acceptanceCriteria: Create apps/app.mento.org/app/components/borrow/shared/flow-step.tsx
Accepts step: FlowStep and isActive: boolean props
Status rendering: idle = circle outline (muted), pending = spinner animation, confirming = spinner + txHash link, confirmed = green checkmark, error = red X icon
When txHash present: render as link to block explorer (check existing codebase for explorer URL pattern — likely celoscan.io or explorer.celo.org)
Error state shows error.message below the step label in red text
Compact vertical layout suitable for stacking in a dialog
Uses Tailwind for all styling (no external icon library — use SVG or unicode symbols)
Typecheck passes (pnpm check-types)
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Create flow dialog component
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see a dialog showing my transaction progress so I know what's happening.
acceptanceCriteria: Create apps/app.mento.org/app/components/borrow/shared/flow-dialog.tsx
Reads borrowFlowAtom from @repo/web3 to get current flow state
Renders as modal/dialog overlay (use @repo/ui Dialog if available, otherwise card with backdrop)
Shows operation name as title
Renders FlowStep for each step in the flow
When all steps confirmed: shows success state with 'Back to Dashboard' button
When a step has error: shows error state with 'Try Again' button
'Back to Dashboard' clears borrowFlowAtom to null and sets borrowViewAtom to 'dashboard'
'Try Again' clears borrowFlowAtom to null (user re-submits from form)
Dialog visible whenever borrowFlowAtom is not null, hidden when null
Typecheck passes (pnpm check-types)
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Integrate flow dialog into borrow-view
userStories.description: As a user, I want the flow dialog to appear automatically when I trigger any borrow transaction.
acceptanceCriteria: Update apps/app.mento.org/app/components/borrow/borrow-view.tsx
Import and render <FlowDialog /> — it self-manages visibility via borrowFlowAtom
FlowDialog renders on top of any borrow sub-view (dashboard, open-trove, manage, earn)
Clicking 'Back to Dashboard' in success state: clears flow atom, navigates to dashboard
Clicking 'Try Again' in error state: clears flow atom, returns to current view
Typecheck passes (pnpm check-types)
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: FlowDialog is a 'fire and forget' component — just render it in borrow-view.tsx and it handles everything via the atom.
